name: Airtable Record
description: An Airtable record representing a single row in a table. Records are the fundamental data units in Airtable, containing field values that correspond to the table's column definitions.

id.description: The unique identifier for the record. Record IDs always start with the 'rec' prefix.

createdTime.description: The date and time when the record was created, in ISO 8601 format.

fields.description: A mapping of field names (or field IDs when returnFieldsByFieldId is enabled) to their cell values. The keys and value types depend on the table's schema definition.
